tex:
\begin{question*}{13}{a}
Jos\'{e} manages a football team.
\newline
 \\
He records the number of goals scored by the team for each of five months.
\newline
 \\
Some of the results are shown on the bar chart.
\newline

\begin{subpartsaliged}
In February, 10 goals were scored.
\newline
 \\
Complete the bar chart.
\newline

Write down the month in which most goals were scored.
\newline

Find the total number of goals scored.
\newline

Calculate the mean number of goals scored each month.
\newline

\end{subpartsaliged}

(b) Jodie and her two children go to a football match.
\newline
\begin{subpartsaliged}
Ticket prices are \$15.30 for an adult and \$6.50 for a child.
\newline
 \\
Calculate the total cost of the three tickets.
\newline

A match programme costs \$3.75.
\newline
 \\
Jodie buys two match programmes.
\newline
 \\
Calculate the change she receives from a \$10 note.
\newline

540 tickets out of 630 are sold for this match.
\newline
 \\
Calculate the percentage of tickets sold.
\newline

The match starts at 14 55 and ends 1 hour 50 minutes later.
\newline
 \\
Work out the time the match ends.
\newline

Jodie travels 66 km to get home after the match.
\newline
 \\
She leaves at 5 pm and arrives home at 6.12 pm.
\newline
 \\
Calculate her average speed in kilometres per hour.
\newline

\end{subpartsaliged}
\end{question*}

\newline

\end{subpartsaliged}

tex:

In the diagram, $A$, $B$ and $C$ are points on the circle, centre $O$.
\newline

\begin{subpartsaliged}
On the diagram, draw a chord.
\newline

Explain why angle $ABC$ is 90$^\circ$.
\newline

\end{subpartsaliged}

(b) The length of the edge of a cube is 8 cm.
\newline
 \\
Calculate the surface area of this cube.
\newline

(c) A cuboid measures 5 cm by 4 cm by 2 cm.
\newline
\begin{subpartsaliged}
Calculate the volume of this cuboid.
\newline
 \\
Give the units of your answer.
\newline

On the 1 cm$^2$ grid, draw an accurate net of this cuboid.
\newline
 \\
One face has been drawn for you.
\newline

\end{subpartsaliged}
\end{question*}

tex:
\begin{question*}{8}{a}

\begin{subpartsaliged}
Measure the size of angle $x$.
\newline

Write down the mathematical name of this type of angle.
\newline

\end{subpartsaliged}

(b) $ABC$ is a straight line.
\newline

Find the value of $y$.
\newline

(c) $QRS$ is an isosceles triangle and $PQR$ is a straight line.
\newline

Find the value of $z$.
\newline

(d) Find the size of one interior angle of a regular octagon.
\newline

\end{question*}

qtype: factors, primes, powers, roots, zero index, fraction operations, LCM, place value
tex:
\begin{question*}{17}{a}
Write the number four hundred and eighteen thousand and seventy two in figures.
\newline

(b) Write down all the factors of 16.
\newline

(c) Write down a prime number between 30 and 40.
\newline

(d) Find the value of
\begin{subpartsaliged}
$\sqrt{729}$,

$18^3$,

$7^0$.
\newline

\end{subpartsaliged}

(e) Saskia has \$600.
\newline
 \\
She spends $\dfrac{1}{5}$ of the \$600 on a coat and gives $\dfrac{1}{3}$ of the \$600 to her son.
\newline
 \\
What fraction of the \$600 does she have left? \\
Give your answer in its simplest form.
\newline

(f) Find the lowest common multiple (LCM) of 15 and 27.
\newline

(g) Write 432 as the product of its prime factors.
\newline

(h) Ella invests \$4000 for 3 years at a rate of 1.2\% per year compound interest.
\newline
 \\
Calculate the value of her investment at the end of the 3 years.
\newline

\end{question*}

content.content:
Write down all the factors of 16.
\newline

Write down a prime number between 30 and 40.
\newline

(d) Find the value of
\begin{subpartsaliged}
$\sqrt{729}$,

$18^3$,

$7^0$.
\newline

\end{subpartsaliged}

(e) Saskia has \$600.
\newline
 \\
She spends $\dfrac{1}{5}$ of the \$600 on a coat and gives $\dfrac{1}{3}$ of the \$600 to her son.
\newline
 \\
What fraction of the \$600 does she have left? \\
Give your answer in its simplest form.
\newline

(f) Find the lowest common multiple (LCM) of 15 and 27.
\newline

(g) Write 432 as the product of its prime factors.
\newline

(h) Ella invests \$4000 for 3 years at a rate of 1.2\% per year compound interest.
\newline
 \\
Calculate the value of her investment at the end of the 3 years.
\newline

\end{question*}

tex:
Triangles $A, B$ and $C$ are shown on the grid.
\newline

(a) Describe fully the single transformation that maps

(i) (b) triangle $A$ onto triangle $B$,

(c) triangle $A$ onto triangle $C$.
\newline

(d) On the grid,

(i) (e) translate triangle $A$ by the vector $\begin{pmatrix} 6 \\ -2 \end{pmatrix}$,

(f) reflect triangle $A$ in the line $y = 1$.
\newline

content.content: Describe fully the single transformation that maps
triangle $A$ onto triangle $B$,

triangle $A$ onto triangle $C$.
\newline
On the grid,
translate triangle $A$ by the vector $\begin{pmatrix} 6 \\ -2 \end{pmatrix}$,

reflect triangle $A$ in the line $y = 1$.
\newline

qtype: finding equation of a straight line from graph
tex:
The line $L$ is shown on the grid.
\newline

(a) Find the equation of the line $L$ in the form $y = mx + c$.
\newline

(b) The equation of a different line is $y = 3x - 4$.
\newline
(i) (c) Write down the gradient of this line.
\newline

(d) Write down the co-ordinates of the point where this line crosses the $y$-axis.
\newline

(e) On the grid, draw the graph of $y = -2x + 1$ for $-4 \le x \le 5$.
\newline

content.content:
Find the equation of the line $L$ in the form $y = mx + c$.
\newline

The equation of a different line is $y = 3x - 4$.
\newline

Write down the gradient of this line.
\newline

Write down the co-ordinates of the point where this line crosses the $y$-axis.
\newline

On the grid, draw the graph of $y = -2x + 1$ for $-4 \le x \le 5$.
\newline

tex:
\begin{question*}{14}{a}
Soraya makes rectangular flags.
\newline

\begin{subpartsaliged}
On the rectangle, draw the lines of symmetry.
\newline
 

Each flag measures 1.2 m by 1.8 m.
\newline
 \\
Calculate the area of one flag.
\newline

\end{subpartsaliged}

(b) Each flag costs \$15 to make.
\newline
 \\
Soraya sells one flag for \$21.
\newline
 \\
Calculate the percentage profit.
\newline

(c) Soraya makes 30 flags.
\newline
 \\
11 flags are pink, 7 are yellow, 5 are blue, 4 are silver and 3 are green.
\newline
 \\
Soraya takes a flag at random.
\newline
 \\
Find the probability that the flag she takes is

\begin{subpartsaliged}
pink,

not blue,

red.
\newline

\end{subpartsaliged}

(d) Soraya decides to make a mathematically similar flag.
\newline

Calculate the height, $h$, of the new flag.
\newline

(e)

The diagram shows a flagpole in Soraya's garden.
\newline
 \\
The flagpole has height 25 m.
\newline
 \\
A rope from the top of the flagpole is tied to the ground 8 m from its base.
\newline
 \\
Calculate the length of this rope.
\newline

\end{question*}

\newline
 \\
Soraya sells one flag for \$21.
\newline
 \\
Calculate the percentage profit.
\newline

\newline
 \\
11 flags are pink, 7 are yellow, 5 are blue, 4 are silver and 3 are green.
\newline
 \\
Soraya takes a flag at random.
\newline
 \\

\begin{subpartsaliged}
pink,

not blue,

red.
\newline

\end{subpartsaliged}

Soraya decides to make a mathematically similar flag.
\newline

\newline

\newline

\newline

A rope from the top of the flagpole is tied to the ground 8 m from its base.
\newline
 \\

\newline

\end{question*}

tex:
\begin{question*}{16}{a}
The scale drawing shows the positions of two buoys, $A$ and $B$, in the sea.
\newline
 \\
The scale is 1 centimetre represents 20 kilometres.
\newline

\begin{subpartsaliged}
Work out the actual distance between buoy $A$ and buoy $B$.
\newline

Measure the bearing of buoy $B$ from buoy $A$.
\newline

Buoy $C$ is 120 km from buoy $B$ on a bearing of 300$^\circ$.
\newline
 \\
On the scale drawing, mark the position of buoy $C$.
\newline

Marco sails his boat so that he is always equidistant from buoy $A$ and buoy $B$.
\newline
 \\
On the scale drawing, use a straight edge and compasses only to construct the path of the boat.
\newline
 \\
Show all your construction arcs.
\newline

\end{subpartsaliged}

(b) The amount of fuel, $t$ litres, in the boat's fuel tank is 135 litres, correct to the nearest litre.
\newline
 \\
Complete the statement about the value of $t$.
\newline

(c) Marco has ropes of four different colours.
\newline
 \\
He takes a rope at random.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|c|}
    \hline
    Colour & Brown & White & Red & Green \\
    \hline
    Probability & 0.35 & & 0.04 & 0.2 \\
    \hline
\end{tabular}
Complete the table.
\newline

(d) When Marco arrives at a port the temperature is 5$^\circ$C.
\newline
 \\
At midnight the temperature has fallen by 7$^\circ$C.
\newline
 \\
Find the temperature at midnight.
\newline

(e) Last year the cost to keep a boat at the port was \$14 per night.
\newline
 \\
This year the cost has increased by 12\%.
\newline
 \\
Calculate the cost this year.
\newline

(f) Marco watched 25 boats enter the port, of which 9 had a mast.
\newline
 \\
There are a total of 200 boats in the port.
\newline
 \\
Calculate an estimate of the number of boats in the port that have a mast.
\newline

\end{question*}

qtype: linear and quadratic sequences, nth term
tex:
\begin{question*}{8}{a}
These are the first four terms of a sequence.
\newline
29 \qquad 32 \qquad 35 \qquad 38
\begin{subpartsaliged}
Write down the next term.
\newline

Write down the rule for continuing this sequence.
\newline

\end{subpartsaliged}

(b) The $n$th term of another sequence is $n^2 + 5$.
\newline
\begin{subpartsaliged}
Find the first three terms.
\newline

Show that 261 is a term in this sequence.
\newline

\end{subpartsaliged}

(c) These are the first four terms of a different sequence.
\newline
27 \qquad 33 \qquad 39 \qquad 45
Find the $n$th term of this sequence.
\newline

\end{question*}
